title: Oxalis cognita
intro: Oxalis cognita, also known as the Yellow Wood Sorrel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Oxalidaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its health benefits, which include the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and gastritis, as well as its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Oxalis cognita include flavonoids, anthraquinones, and oxalic acid, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas and infusions, as well as a tincture, which are traditionally used to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Oxalis cognita is a species of the plant genus Oxalis, which belongs to the family Oxalidaceae. This family is part of the order Oxalidales, and further classified under the subclass Rosidae, which is a part of the class Magnoliopsida in the kingdom Plantae. Oxalis cognita is often categorized as an herbaceous perennial plant. The Oxalis genus is native to the Americas, with some species being found in Africa and other parts of the world. Oxalis species are often known for their unique, intricate flowers and ability to thrive in a variety of environments.
